title: ruscus aculeatus subsp. laxus
intro:
Ruscus aculeatus subsp. laxus, also known as Butcher's broom, is a type of evergreen shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its clusters of small, yellowish-green flowers and dark green, glossy leaves.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties, which are derived from its saponin content.

The health benefits of Ruscus aculeatus subsp. laxus include improved circulation, reduced swelling and pain, and enhanced immune function, making it a popular remedy for conditions such as varicose veins, hemorrhoids, and arthritis.

Ruscus aculeatus subsp. laxus is commonly used in herbal preparations, including tea, tinctures, and capsules, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
